title: T-012 — Warehouse RLS policies + org isolation audit (WH §3, §16.5, ADR-003/013)
prompt:
# T-012 — Warehouse RLS policies + org isolation audit (WH §3, §16.5, ADR-003/013)

PRD: docs/prd/05-WAREHOUSE-PRD.md §3, §16.5
Project root: /Users/mariuszkrawczyk/Projects/monopilot-kira

## Goal
Verify and lock RLS policies on every warehouse org-scoped table (license_plates, lp_genealogy, lp_reservations, grns, grn_items, stock_moves, pick_overrides, shelf_life_rules, warehouse_settings) and add a cross-table integration test proving zero cross-org data leakage. Policies use the foundation `app.current_org_id()` function (see `packages/db/migrations/002-rls-baseline.sql`).

## Implementation contract
1. Migration `0NN_warehouse_rls_audit.sql`: ensure `ENABLE ROW LEVEL SECURITY` + `<table>_org_context` policy `USING (org_id = app.current_org_id()) WITH CHECK (org_id = app.current_org_id())` on all 9 tables. Never use `current_setting('app.tenant_id')` or `current_setting('app.current_org_id')` GUC.
2. Add `FORCE ROW LEVEL SECURITY` so the table owner cannot bypass policies.
3. Add integration test `packages/db/test/warehouse/rls-isolation.test.ts` seeding 2 orgs × 9 tables and asserting org_B sees 0 of org_A's rows for every read path (tests connect as `app_user` and call `app.set_org_context(session_token, org_id)` per org).

## Files
- packages/db/migrations/0NN_warehouse_rls.sql [create]
- packages/db/src/schema/__tests__/warehouse-rls.test.ts [create]

## Acceptance criteria
1. Given two orgs seed identical row counts across all 9 warehouse tables, when org_B's session (post `app.set_org_context(session_token, org_B)`) reads any table, then exactly org_B's rows are returned (zero leakage).
2. Given no `app.set_org_context(...)` has been called in the current transaction (so `app.current_org_id()` returns NULL), when any warehouse table is queried, then no rows are returned (fail-closed).
3. Given a superuser bypass attempt, when `SET row_security = off` is tried inside a non-superuser session, then it fails (FORCE RLS).
4. Given pg_policies is inspected, when warehouse policies are listed, then every qual/withcheck references `app.current_org_id()` and contains no `current_setting('app.tenant_id'|'app.current_org_id')` GUC.

## Test strategy
- RED: `packages/db/test/warehouse/rls-isolation.test.ts` covering every table × every read path; tests use app_user connection + app.set_org_context per org.
- Run `pnpm --filter db migrate && pnpm --filter db test rls-isolation`.

## Risk red lines
- Do not read `current_setting('app.tenant_id')` or `current_setting('app.current_org_id')` directly — use the foundation `app.current_org_id()` function so the NULL-safe setter contract is preserved.
- Do not use `tenant_id` as the business-scope column; per Wave0 v4.3 lock it is `org_id`.
- Do not skip FORCE ROW LEVEL SECURITY.
- Do not weaken policies to allow cross-org reads under any role.
- Do not rely solely on app-layer filtering — DB must enforce.

## Dependencies
- T-002
- T-003
- T-004
- T-005
- T-006
- T-007
- T-008
- T-009
